steps.title: Use the arc fraction
steps.content: An arc's length is the same fraction of the circumference as the arc's degree measure is of $360^{\circ}$. Set up a proportion using the $60^{\circ}$ arc and its $4$-inch length.

steps.title: Set up the arc length formula
steps.content:
The length of an arc is proportional to its central angle. When a central angle spans $\theta$ degrees out of the full $360^{\circ}$, the arc length is:
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times C$$
where $C$ is the circumference of the circle.

steps.title: Substitute values and solve for circumference
steps.content:
We have an arc measuring $60^{\circ}$ with length $4$ inches. Substitute these values:
$$4 = \frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times C$$
Simplify the fraction $\frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} = \frac{1}{6}$:
$$4 = \frac{1}{6} \times C$$
Multiply both sides by $6$:
$$C = 24 \text{ inches}$$
That is the correct answer.
